title: Onboarding crashes in prod release: cwd-relative priv path in starter_data
description: starter_data.ex:93 does File.read!(priv/recipe_catalog.json) with a cwd-relative path. Works in dev; in a release cwd differs and priv lives under the app lib dir -> File.Error, so new-account onboarding fails in production. Use Application.app_dir(:grocery_planner, ...).
acceptance_criteria:
- Path resolved via Application.app_dir
- Test asserts catalog loads via the app-dir path
- Grep confirms no other cwd-relative priv reads in lib/

title: Recipe import creates duplicate grocery items (nil-actor silent empty read) and swallows ingredient failures
description: recipe_importer.ex:60 calls Inventory.list_grocery_items(tenant: account_id) with no actor and no authorize?: false - the documented actor-nil pitfall: relates_to_actor_via policy silently returns [], so find_or_create never finds and every import duplicates grocery items. Compare correct callers item_matcher.ex:110 and receipt_live.ex:1093. Additionally import_ingredients (recipe_importer.ex:32-54) runs a no-else with inside Enum.each, discards create_recipe_ingredient results, and returns :ok unconditionally - recipes import as success with missing ingredients.
acceptance_criteria:
- list_grocery_items call passes authorize?: false (or real actor)
- Re-importing the same recipe twice does not duplicate grocery items (test)
- Ingredient creation failures surface as an error result from import_recipe (test)

title: Email digest is non-functional 3 ways: unsupervised GenServer, TenantRequired read, bad Ash.load - fix or delete
description: Notifications.EmailScheduler (notifications/email_scheduler.ex) never runs: (1) GenServer absent from application.ex supervision tree; (2) send_daily_emails/0 reads multi-tenant NotificationPreference with no tenant and no allow_global -> raises TenantRequired (lines 43-45); (3) Ash.load! called on never-loaded associations preference.user/preference.account -> raises, also N+1 (lines 48-49; intent was Ash.load!(preference, [:user, :account])). Zero test coverage is why three stacked defects went unnoticed. Decide: fix and supervise it, or delete the module (303 lines).
acceptance_criteria:
- Decision recorded: keep or delete
- If keep: supervised in application.ex, tenant-correct read (allow_global read action or per-tenant iteration), associations loaded correctly, test proving a digest email is generated for a user with due notifications
- If delete: module and dead config removed

title: Fix member role escalation: role-gate AccountMembership/User policies + settings_live guard
description: Any :member of a shared account can add a colluding :owner and remove the real owner (settings_live.ex:200-253 never checks actor role; role comes from params). Root cause: AccountMembership has blanket 'policy always()' (accounts/account_membership.ex:37-41) and User is unscoped (accounts/user.ex:104-108 - any actor can update any user email). ActorMemberOfAccount check already exists and is used on Recipe/Family resources. Not reachable via JSON API (Accounts domain not mounted) but directly clickable in the UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- AccountMembership create/update/destroy require actor to be owner/admin of the target account (policy-level)
- User read/update/destroy scoped to self
- settings_live send_invitation/remove_member pass actor and enforce role
- Test: a :member attempting to add an owner or remove the owner gets Forbidden
- Existing owner/admin flows still pass

title: Focus mode copy_previous_day never works on Mondays (week-cache miss)
description: focus_layout.ex copy_previous_day read source-day meals from socket.assigns.week_meals, which only holds the displayed week. When selected_day is the first day of the week (every Monday), yesterday belongs to the previous week, the cache lookup returns nothing, and the user gets 'No meals found on previous day' even when Sunday had meals. Side effect: the date-dependent test 'copies yesterday's meals to today' failed every Monday UTC, blocking all CI merges that day. Fixed by querying list_meal_plans_by_date_range for the source day instead of the cache, plus a deterministic regression test that pins the Monday case by selecting the week's first day explicitly.
close_reason: Fixed in same PR: copy_previous_day now queries list_meal_plans_by_date_range for the source day instead of the week-scoped cache; deterministic regression test pins the Monday/week-boundary case. Was also blocking all Monday-UTC CI runs via the date-dependent test.

title: Data portability: full export + Mealie/Tandoor/Paprika importers
description: Yummly died Dec 2024 with no bulk export - users lost everything; PlateJoy followed in 2025. Portability is now the loudest category grievance and a self-hosted app should own it on principle: one-click full export (recipes, plans, inventory, preferences) in open formats (JSON + schema.org recipes), and importers for Mealie/Tandoor/Paprika archives - which is simultaneously the adoption funnel FROM those apps. Cheap, principled, marketable.

title: Leftovers as first-class inventory entries
description: Leftovers are one of the biggest waste contributors and invisible to nearly every planner - we are halfway there with leftover chains (base -> follow-up recipes, usage_type :leftover). Close the loop: completing a meal with extra servings creates a leftovers inventory entry (short expiry, fridge location) that shows in eat-me-first, satisfies follow-up-recipe ingredients, and can be planned directly as tomorrow's lunch (eat-leftovers slot type). Only niche apps (Eat This Much, SideChef cook-once-eat-twice) touch this.
notes: Evidence upgrade from final competitor deep-dive: cooked-leftover INVENTORY (portions + cooked-on/use-by) is near-total whitespace ranked #2 best-evidenced opportunity. Demand is GitHub-validated (Grocy #942 closed unimplemented, #1181 portion-scaling broken; Samsung Food review: cannot track leftovers; 2025 food-waste roundup names the gap). Scheduling a leftover NIGHT is solved (Plan to Eat); the cooked-dish-as-dated-portioned-inventory half is the gap we should own. Raised P2->P1.

title: Meal-plan templates UI (backend fully built, zero UI)
description: MealPlanTemplate + entries are fully modelled server-side with CRUD, activate/deactivate, and sync/JSON-API exposure - but no screen creates, names, or applies one. Cheapest feature on this list: save-current-week-as-template, apply-template-to-week (respecting occupied slots), template library. Solves the rigidity pain point (rotating known-good weeks beats replanning from scratch) and un-strands sync-ready backend work.

title: Aisle/section-ordered shopping lists per store
description: Now a baseline expectation - AnyList, Mealime, Plan to Eat, Tandoor, even Apple Grocery sort by store section; reviewers describe unsorted lists as broken (crisscrossing the store turns 20 minutes into an hour). Category-to-section mapping per named store (our categories already exist), drag-to-reorder sections to match the local shop, group same-ingredient lines (all cheeses together). Optionally learn order from check-off sequence over time.

title: Personal item price history from receipts
description: The genuine market gap: comparison sites (Trolley.co.uk) track shelf prices, but nobody shows what YOUR household actually paid over time - and we already capture the raw material in receipt line items with prices and dates. Per-item price timeline (you paid 1.10 -> 1.35 -> 1.60 over 8 months), sparkline on the item page, per-store comparison from receipt merchants. Private-by-construction - a cloud app cannot credibly offer this. AnyList-style price tracking is one of the few features users pay for. Depends on the receipt pipeline being robust (review finding cxk).

title: Finish email invitations: invite non-registered members to the household
description: The invite flow only attaches already-registered users by email lookup; inviting an actual new person (send email -> they sign up -> auto-join household with chosen role) is unbuilt. Family participation is pain-point number 8 - the enthusiast adopts, the household does not - and this is the literal front gate. Low effort, completes an existing half-feature, prerequisite for every household-coordination idea below.

title: Tonight screen: expiry- and family-aware dinner suggestions as the front door
description: The number-one job in every pain-point source is what is for dinner tonight with what I have - decision fatigue kills weekly-grid planners. We already have all the pieces buried inside the meal planner (waste-watch, cook-with-these, MealOptimizer scoring, family preference set-cover); elevate them into the default landing experience: 3 suggestions ranked by expiring inventory x on-hand coverage x family compatibility, one tap to plan or start cooking, an eat-me-first strip of items to use up. Optional daily push/email at 4pm. Turns the app from a planning tool into a daily answer machine.

title: Frictionless pantry mode: on-hand flags, loose quantities, auto-decrement on cooked meals
description: Inventory upkeep is the category-killer: Grocy drowns users in data entry (reviewers: a very demanding data entry job), Mealie refuses to build pantry tracking at all. The winning wedge is inventory as a passive byproduct: half-a-pack granularity instead of exact counts, an on-hand toggle per item, auto-decrement of ingredients when a planned meal is marked completed, and fully wiring the existing fuzzy ItemMatcher into receipt confirmation (currently exact-name only). We already own the hard part (receipt OCR); this makes the data trustworthy without discipline.

title: Recipe import from any URL and social video (with embedding dedupe)
description: Table stakes everywhere (Paprika, Mealie, Tandoor, Samsung Food all have it) yet we import only from TheMealDB - the single biggest feature gap vs the whole category. Recipes actually live in Instagram/TikTok/YouTube/blogs/photos; imports that work there are the most-shipped, most-loved AI food feature of 2025-26 (Mealie ships video transcription -> LLM extraction). Our edge: dedupe and cross-link imports against existing pgvector embeddings so the household corpus stays clean (nobody else does import-dedupe). Pipeline: schema.org scraper first, AI-sidecar LLM extraction fallback for video/social/photo.

title: Add family_live LiveView tests (FM-002 has zero UI coverage)
description: family_live.ex (294 lines, the FM-002 meal-time-solution feature from the latest commits) has no LiveView test at all - only domain-level unit tests exist. The suite already has the right pattern (mount -> interact -> assert, see receipt_live_test.exs). Cover: mount renders members, click-to-exclude member in meal time solution, preference toggling, 2-state preference model rendering.
acceptance_criteria:
- test/grocery_planner_web/live/family_live_test.exs exercises mount + exclusion click + preference toggle via render_click
- Uses existing FamilyTestHelpers (production code paths)

title: Configure prod OpenTelemetry exporter (currently exporting to nowhere)
description: application.ex:12 calls setup_opentelemetry() unconditionally, but an exporter endpoint is configured only in config/dev.exs:96-98 (set to :none). In prod the exporter falls back to localhost:4317 and every span is silently dropped - full instrumentation cost, zero visibility. docker-compose.yml:24 sets OTEL_ENABLED only on the python service; the Elixir app reads nothing. Add config :opentelemetry_exporter, otlp_endpoint: System.get_env(...) under prod in runtime.exs and gate setup_opentelemetry() on OTEL_ENABLED so it no-ops without a collector.
acceptance_criteria:
- Prod exporter endpoint env-configurable; setup gated on OTEL_ENABLED
- With no collector configured, OTel setup no-ops (no background export errors)
- With a collector configured, spans arrive (verified against homelab collector or documented as pending one)

title: Prevent duplicate meal-plan slots: unique index + occupied-slot handling on all add paths
description: MealPlan has no identity/unique index on (account_id, scheduled_date, meal_type). perform_add_meal (meal_planner_live.ex:1490 area), drop_recipe (:553), and copy_last_week all create without checking slot occupancy, and data_loader.ex:41 masks duplicates via Enum.into keyed on meal_type - hidden orphan rows accumulate. Add an Ash identity (partial unique index respecting deleted_at soft-deletes), handle the conflict in the create paths (replace or reject), and make data_loader surface rather than mask.
acceptance_criteria:
- Migration adds partial unique index on (account_id, scheduled_date, meal_type) where deleted_at is null
- Adding to an occupied slot has defined behavior (replace or friendly error) on every path incl copy_last_week (tests)
- Data cleanup for existing duplicate rows handled in the migration or a task

title: Serve receipt images through authenticated tenant-scoped controller instead of Plug.Static
description: Receipt images (purchase PII) are written to priv/static/uploads/receipts/ (receipt_processor.ex:44-61) and served by Plug.Static via the uploads entry in static_paths (grocery_planner_web.ex:20) with no auth or tenant check. UUID filenames make guessing impractical but any leaked URL exposes another tenant receipt to anyone. Store outside priv/static and serve via a controller that verifies the actor belongs to the receipt account; re-validate extension server-side in store_file/1 (currently client-derived Path.extname).
acceptance_criteria:
- Unauthenticated request for a receipt image gets 401/403/404
- Authenticated user of a different account gets 403/404 (test)
- Files stored outside priv/static; upload extension validated server-side
- Existing receipt display flows still work

title: API bearer tokens: add revocation - survive logout and password reset for 24h
description: plugs/api_auth.ex:16 verifies Phoenix.Token with max_age 86_400 and payload of just user.id. No server-side revocation: a stolen token stays valid through logout AND password reset (api/auth_controller.ex:9 mints; reset flow rotates nothing). Options: embed a per-user token_version checked on verify and bumped on logout/password-reset, or a server-side token store; also consider shortening max_age.
acceptance_criteria:
- Password reset invalidates previously issued API tokens (test)
- Logout (or an explicit revoke endpoint) invalidates tokens (test)
- Decision on max_age recorded

title: Enable force_ssl/HSTS and Postgres TLS in production
description: No force_ssl anywhere (config/runtime.exs:110-116 has it only in comments) and Repo ssl is commented out (runtime.exs:45). Session cookie and API bearer tokens are capturable via downgrade/MITM; DB traffic incl hashed_password travels unencrypted. Add force_ssl: [hsts: true] to the prod endpoint and ssl: true to the Repo config (verify the homelab Postgres accepts TLS first).
acceptance_criteria:
- Prod endpoint sets force_ssl with HSTS
- Repo connects with TLS in prod
- Deploy verified: app boots and serves over https, DB connection healthy

title: Deduplicate receipt-item-to-inventory logic: price field drift and hardcoded USD
description: The create-inventory-entry-from-receipt-item logic is duplicated and has drifted: receipts_live.ex:740-772 uses item.unit_price for purchase_price while receipt_processor.ex:351-379 uses item.total_price - the same receipt imported via /receipts vs /receipts/scan records different prices. parse_flat_money (receipt_processor.ex:403-424) also hardcodes :USD (app default is :GBP) and builds Money from raw JSON floats. Extract one shared function with one price policy and currency from config.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single shared function used by both paths
- Explicit decision on unit_price vs total_price recorded in the code
- Currency comes from app config, not a literal
- Test covering both import paths producing identical entries

title: Add anti-corruption layer for AI sidecar responses (categorizer + embeddings) and resolve dead Contracts module
description: ai/categorizer.ex trusts Python-service JSON raw with concrete crash vectors: String.to_existing_atom on server strings (230, 245), is_float-guarded confidence fed JSON ints/nulls (190-224), single vs batch paths derive confidence_level differently; embeddings.ex:31-46 has no clause for empty embeddings list. Meanwhile contracts.ex - built to validate exactly this boundary - is never called anywhere (dead code). Also fold in: ai_client.ex:152 ignores the :ai_service_url runtime config and re-reads env directly (runtime.exs:76-78 is dead config), and ai_client.ex:188-190 logs full response bodies (receipt PII) at error level. Model the normalizer on the_meal_db.ex parse_meal/1.
acceptance_criteria:
- Normalizing parser validates category, coerces confidence to float with default, maps confidence_level defensively; malformed payloads degrade with error tuples, never raise (tests with int/null/unknown inputs)
- Contracts module either wired in or deleted
- ai_client reads Application.get_env(:grocery_planner, :ai_service_url)
- Error logs no longer include full response bodies

title: Wire recipe embedding AshOban trigger and delete raw-SQL workaround workers
description: The declarative ash_ai_update_embeddings trigger (recipes/recipe.ex:88) never fires: application.ex:18 hands AshOban only [Inventory] while config.exs:84 lists [Inventory, Recipes]. Recipes are never auto-embedded; semantic search silently degrades until manual backfill. The raw-SQL workers (workers/embedding_worker.ex:22-33, embedding_backfill_worker.ex) exist only as a workaround, bypass Ash/tenancy/field-contract, and have zero tests. Add Recipes to AshOban.config in application.ex, verify the trigger enqueues on create/update, then remove the manual workers (or rewrite any remaining manual path through the Recipe resource with authorize?: false + tenant).
acceptance_criteria:
- Creating/updating a recipe enqueues an embedding job (test)
- Raw-SQL workers deleted or rewritten through Ash
- Backfill path still available and tested
- application.ex and config.exs domain lists agree

title: Receipt worker robustness: stuck Processing on failure, duplicate items on retry, premature completed status
description: Three related defects in receipt_processor.ex under the AshOban :process trigger: (1) line 112 hard-matches {:ok, _} = update_receipt - any validation failure raises, no receipt_failed broadcast, UI spins on Processing forever; (2) lines 131-134 create items with no idempotency - Oban retry after partial success duplicates line items; (3) status flips to :completed before items persist, so a mid-run crash leaves a completed receipt with partial items (98-145). Feature is flag-gated off today; fix before flipping receipt_processing on.
acceptance_criteria:
- Update failures produce a failed status + receipt_failed broadcast (test)
- Item creation idempotent under retry: re-running the worker yields no duplicates (test)
- Status set to completed only after items persist
- check_duplicate (line 90) fails closed on query error

title: Email digest rebuild-or-remove: wire daily digest as Oban cron worker, or drop the dead email toggle
description: Follow-up to grocery_planner-4a4, where the never-run Notifications.EmailScheduler GenServer (broken 3 ways, zero references, zero tests) was DELETED rather than fixed. What remains is a product gap: settings exposes email_notifications_enabled on NotificationPreference and users can enable it, but nothing sends email - a dead promise in the UI. Two options: (A) Rebuild the digest properly: Oban cron worker (Cron plugin already configured in config.exs with an empty job list), per-tenant reads via allow_global read action or per-account iteration with set_tenant, Ash.load!(pref, [:user, :account]) done correctly (single load, no N+1), send time derived from the account's timezone attribute instead of hardcoded 7AM UTC, from-address from config not hardcoded notifications@groceryplanner.com, and a test proving a digest is generated for a user with due notifications. The old HTML template and summary/recipe-row builders are recoverable from git history (lib/grocery_planner/notifications/email_scheduler.ex, deleted in the 4a4 fix). ExpirationAlerts and RecipeSuggestions modules still exist and are used by dashboard/meal planner. (B) Remove the email_notifications_enabled toggle from settings UI and the NotificationPreference resource until email digests are actually built. Decide based on whether email digest is on the product roadmap (see notifications backlog).
acceptance_criteria: Decision A or B recorded and implemented; no UI toggle exists that silently does nothing

title: Prep-day and freezer-meal support: batch cook, portion, drag back into the plan
description: Plan to Eat holds a near-monopoly on prepped-meal freezer inventory (Add to Freezer: count, servings, date; drag portions back onto future plan days) - absent from Mealime, Samsung Food, Mealie, Tandoor, Grocy, Paprika. Distinct from incidental leftovers (grocery_planner-def): this is DELIBERATE batch cooking - plan a prep day, cook 4x, freeze dated portions as inventory (freezer storage locations and freezable recipe flags already exist), then schedule those portions into future weeks. Completes the batch-cooking trio with servings scaling (cook mode bead) and cook-once-eat-many chains (already shipped).

title: Recipe nutrition via Open Food Facts: bounded macros, no medical claims
description: Auto-compute per-serving macros for recipes by joining ingredients to Open Food Facts nutrition data (keyed off existing categorization/barcodes), show per-recipe and per-planned-week totals, filter by high-protein etc. Research boundary: basic macro display is table stakes, but condition-specific medical planning (diabetic/FODMAP/renal) is explicitly deferred - LLM nutrition errors of 36-53 percent make it a liability without clinical framing. Feeds the natural-language planner's nutritional constraints.
notes: Priority raised P3->P2 and scope sharpened by final competitor deep-dive: the best-evidenced whitespace in the entire 10-capability analysis is the DAILY/WEEKLY NUTRITION ROLL-UP of the planned week vs a household goal (demand: Mealie #3743 top request, Grocy #413; supply: absent even in category leaders; PlateJoy did it and died for unrelated reasons). Per-recipe macros are the substrate; the plan-level roll-up vs goal is the differentiator. Also: import nutrition alongside the recipe when scraping (Tandoor #3391 - usually dropped by importers).

title: Mobile companion app on the existing offline-sync API
description: The purpose-built sync layer (delta pull, tombstones, atomic batch, optimistic concurrency, temp-id reconciliation) is a client contract waiting for a client. The recurring OSS ask is a mobile app / robust offline PWA - recipes and lists usable in-store and in-kitchen without signal. Decide the vehicle (installable PWA with local cache vs LiveView Native vs Flutter) and ship the two killer offline surfaces first: shopping list check-off and tonight's recipe. Sync reliability is what family-coordination apps live or die on - and the hard part is already built.

title: Home Assistant integration: sensors, notifications, Assist intents
description: Research verdict: arguably the highest-leverage single feature for the self-hosted family audience - Grocy's popularity is substantially BECAUSE of its HA add-on. Expose expiring-items count, low-stock list, tonight's planned meal, and shopping-list summary as HA sensors (REST/MQTT); push eat-me-first and expiry alerts through HA notifications to the devices the family already has; an Assist intent for what-can-I-make-tonight keeps voice local and private (vs Alexa in the kitchen). Plugs us into the hub this audience already runs.

title: Unit conversion + ingredient merge/dedup tooling
description: Recurring top-10 OSS request (Mealie 2024 survey): the same ingredient stored five ways breaks inventory matching, shopping aggregation, and can-I-make-this calculations. Admin merge tool for duplicate grocery items/units (re-pointing ingredients and entries), metric/imperial conversion on display and on shopping aggregation, normalization suggestions at import time (import creates chicken breast; you have Chicken Breasts - merge?). Data hygiene that quietly makes every other feature more accurate.

title: Cook mode: screen-awake guided steps with multi-timers and scaling
description: Paprika's most-loved in-kitchen feature: full-screen step-by-step instructions that keep the phone/tablet awake, inline multi-timers parsed from step text (simmer 20 min -> tap to start), ingredient quantities scaled to planned servings, step check-off. Makes the app present at the stove, not just at the desk - and is pure LiveView UI over data we already have.

title: Recipe cost per serving and cheap-tonight filter
description: Price each recipe from the household's own paid prices (fall back to Open Prices community data): cost per serving on recipe cards, week-plan total on the grocery-impact preview, and a sort-by-cheapest filter. Verbatim OSS-tracker use case: the just-before-paycheque days dinner pick. Tandoor has a basic meal-cost calc; doing it from actual personal receipt prices is the differentiated version.

title: Household grocery inflation dashboard with shrinkflation flags
description: Your personal CPI: household spend up 9 percent YoY driven by dairy - computed from your own receipts, not national averages. 80 percent of consumers rank grocery inflation a top concern; Trolley.co.uk publishes a market-wide index but nobody offers a private household one. Shrinkflation flags where pack size is captured (price flat, size down - Open Food Facts Open Prices was built partly for this). Extends the existing analytics screen; ties waste stats to real money (your binned salad costs 12/month).

title: Modular split-the-pot recipes: shared base + per-member forks
description: 85 percent of US households have at least one member with a dietary requirement; families report cooking multiple dinners nightly. No mainstream planner models a recipe as shared base + per-person variants (bolognese base -> meat/lentil forks; build-your-own-bowl components). We are uniquely positioned: the 2-state preference model and MealTimeSolution set-cover already reason about who eats what - extend the recipe model with component/variant structure and teach the planner and shopping list to aggregate across forks. Clear whitespace nobody owns.

title: Voting v2: no-login quick-tap ballots, recurring vote nights, preference-aware options
description: Voting exists end-to-end (sessions, tally, auto-distribution into next week) but requires each member to log in - and family non-participation is pain-point number 8. Add magic-link quick-tap ballots (tap 3 emoji-sized recipe cards from a phone, no account gauntlet), scheduled recurring sessions (Friday-night vote for next week), and candidate lists pre-filtered by the 2-state preference model so nobody votes for a meal someone will not eat. Make participation cheaper than complaining.

title: Cooking assignment: claimable who-cooks-Tuesday slots with fair-share view
description: Competitor matrix verdict: chore-like cooking assignment is done by essentially nobody, yet meal planning being one parent's burden is a top abandonment reason. Add an assignee to meal plans: members claim (or are assigned) cook duty per meal, plan views show who cooks, a light fair-share tally shows the split over time. Extends the existing meal plan model + membership roles; pairs with the voting feature for a full family loop.
notes: Evidence from final competitor deep-dive: clean whitespace confirmed - NO meal-planning app assigns a specific cook to a specific meal-plan entry (Mealie #4157 unbuilt; Grocy has a chore engine that never references recipes/meal-plan; Skylight nearest with free-text tasks). Ranked #3 best-evidenced opportunity.

title: Natural-language week planning (RAG over own recipes + inventory + family constraints)
description: Plan me a cheap, high-protein week that uses up the chicken - LLM constraint-juggling is genuinely good at this and it is shipping in Melio/Ollie/FoodiePrep. Critical design decision from research: compose ONLY from the household vetted recipe corpus + current inventory (RAG via existing pgvector embeddings), never free-generate recipes - AI-generated cooking instructions have documented safety failures (near-botulism advice, 36-53 percent nutrition errors). Auto-fill for the week already exists; this is its conversational, constraint-aware upgrade. Depends on embeddings actually generating (review finding).

title: Auto shelf-life estimation on receipt and barcode import
description: Expiry data powers waste-watch, eat-me-first, and alerts - but users will not type use-by dates per item. Maintain category-level default shelf-life windows (dairy 7d, frozen 90d, tinned 2y; user-tunable), applied automatically when receipt items or barcode scans become inventory entries, with storage-location awareness (freezer multiplies shelf life). Turns the existing expiry machinery from manually-fed to self-populating.

title: Barcode add/consume path via Open Food Facts
description: Receipts cover the big shop; barcode covers one-offs, restocks, and consume-on-open. Phone-camera scan in the web app/PWA -> Open Food Facts lookup (free API) for name/category/image/nutrition -> one-tap add or decrement. Optional ESPHome hardware scanner endpoint for the homelab crowd (established Grocy pattern with real community energy). Avoid the Grocy mistake of gating scanning behind painful HTTPS/mobile setup.

title: Fill remaining test coverage gaps: receipts list, recipe form/show LiveViews, Voting module
description: Modules with zero test references (verified by grep, not filename matching): receipts_live.ex (the list page - only the scan flow has tests), recipe_form_live.ex, recipe_show_live.ex, and MealPlanning.Voting (core voting business logic - only resource-level vote_session/vote_entry tests exist). Lower priority sweeps: RecipeTag, FilterPreset, ReceiptItem, GroceryItemTagging, UserEmail - confirm no business logic hiding untested. EmailScheduler coverage is handled in grocery_planner-4a4; embedding workers in grocery_planner-z7h.
acceptance_criteria:
- LiveView tests (mount -> interact -> assert) for receipts list, recipe form, recipe show
- Unit tests for Voting core paths (session lifecycle, finalize)
- Quick pass over the thin resources confirming trivial or adding a test

title: Replace Process.sleep timing hacks in tests with assert_eventually
description: Flaky-under-load patterns: offline_sync_test.exs:207 sleeps 1100ms to push updated_at past a since boundary (plus :237,239 10ms sleeps for ordering); sync_pull_test.exs:127 same 1100ms pattern; inventory_live_categorization_test.exs:83 bare sleep(100) then single render check for an async call. The suite already contains the right helper - assert_eventually with bounded retry/backoff in receipt_live_test.exs:93-112. Extract it to a shared support module and reuse; for the timestamp-boundary tests prefer setting updated_at explicitly through an update action or injected clock over wall-clock sleeps.
acceptance_criteria:
- assert_eventually extracted to test/support and reused
- No bare Process.sleep remains in the three named files
- Suite time reduced by the removed 1.1s sleeps

title: Rewrite grocery_impact_test to use production create actions instead of Ash.Seed
description: grocery_impact_test.exs:8-116 is the only file in the suite using Ash.Seed.seed! (10 occurrences: Account, User, AccountMembership, GroceryItem, InventoryEntry, Recipe, RecipeIngredient, MealPlan) - bypasses all validations/changes/defaults, e.g. hashed_password: hash is not a real bcrypt hash. Violates the fixture-honesty rule. Rewrite with the existing test/support helpers (MealPlanning/Inventory/RecipesTestHelpers) which all route through real domain actions.
acceptance_criteria:
- No Ash.Seed usage remains in the file
- Tests still pass using the shared helpers
- While there: delete dead empty test/support/mocks.ex and consider extracting the 5x-duplicated create_account/create_user into a shared helper

title: Sign-in LiveView test only asserts render - add real submission coverage
description: sign_in_live_test.exs is 12 lines / 1 test asserting the page renders. No test of correct credentials -> session + redirect, wrong password -> error, unknown email -> same timing-safe error. Sign-in is the most security-relevant flow and has the thinnest coverage (compare sign_up 88 lines, reset_password 117 lines).
acceptance_criteria:
- Successful sign-in test: form submit -> redirect + authenticated session
- Wrong-password and unknown-email tests assert the same generic error

title: Small security hardening batch: CSP header, signing_salt to env
description: (1) No Content-Security-Policy: router.ex:10 put_secure_browser_headers sets defaults only - add a CSP via its custom-headers map (needs LiveView websocket + inline style allowances worked out). (2) Hardcoded signing_salt committed (endpoint.ex:11 WvMBSdBC, config/config.exs:95) - low risk since secret_key_base is env-sourced, but move salts to runtime env.
acceptance_criteria:
- CSP header present on browser responses; LiveView still connects
- Salts read from env in runtime.exs with safe defaults for dev/test

title: CI gates: mix ash.codegen --check and mix_audit
description: Two missing CI gates: (1) mix ash.codegen --check so Ash snapshot/migration drift fails the build instead of surfacing at deploy (31 migrations / 53 snapshots, drift unverifiable by eye); (2) no CVE scanner in the dep tree - hex.audit only catches retired packages. Add mix_audit dep and run both in CI.
acceptance_criteria:
- CI fails on uncommitted codegen drift (verified by intentionally dirtying once)
- mix deps.audit runs in CI
- mix precommit updated if appropriate

title: Health endpoint: treat optional AI sidecar as degraded, not fatal; add compose healthcheck
description: health_controller.ex:51 maps AI-service-down to degraded and :22 returns 503 for any non-ok - so an outage of the flag-gated-off AI sidecar reports the whole app unhealthy. Also docker-compose.yml:54-56 makes app STARTUP hard-depend on python-service service_healthy, and the elixir-app service has no healthcheck of its own. Split liveness from readiness, treat AI as non-fatal (200 + degraded flag), relax the startup dependency, add a compose healthcheck for the app.
acceptance_criteria:
- AI down -> 200 with degraded flag; DB down -> 503
- App boots without the python sidecar
- elixir-app has a compose healthcheck hitting the readiness endpoint

title: Push filtering/pagination into Ash queries (recipes, shopping, inventory tabs)
description: recipes_live.ex:220-257, shopping_live.ex:475-498, and inventory tabs fetch the full result set on every event (every search keystroke re-queries all recipes) and paginate in memory with Enum.drop/take. Keyset pagination already exists on Recipe :read. Move search filters and pagination into the Ash query per screen. Related small wins can ride along: voting_active? lists all vote sessions in memory in every mount of 3 LiveViews (voting.ex:11-31 - push status filter into query); build_dislike_map does one query per member (meal_time_solution.ex:143-152 - use the bulk read); exclude/include_member re-fetch the recipe per toggle (recipes_live.ex:196-218).
acceptance_criteria:
- Search + pagination server-side on recipes, shopping, inventory
- voting_active? filters status in the query
- build_dislike_map collapses to a single read
- UI behavior unchanged (LiveView tests pass)

title: Receipt item matching: ~30 full-catalog scans per receipt, synchronous in handle_info
description: item_matcher.ex:33-52,109-110: find_best_match runs per item; fuzzy_match reads the entire grocery catalog via list_grocery_items for EVERY item plus exact/normalized point lookups - a 30-line receipt does ~30 full scans + ~60 lookups, all synchronously inside ReceiptLive handle_info (receipt_live.ex:998), blocking the socket. Load the catalog once per receipt and pass it through; move matching to start_async.
acceptance_criteria:
- Catalog fetched once per matching run
- Matching runs off the LiveView process (start_async or task) with a loading state
- Match results unchanged (existing tests pass)

title: load_week_meals loads entire meal-plan history on every navigation
description: data_loader.ex:15-34 loads ALL meal plans for the account then filters to the week in memory (the code comment admits it) on every navigation/refresh; grows unbounded with history. The by_date_range action already exists on MealPlan and is unused here.
acceptance_criteria:
- load_week_meals uses by_date_range
- Existing meal planner tests pass; week rendering unchanged

title: Eliminate LiveView crash class: hard-matches and unvalidated params on client events
description: Recurring pattern: {:ok, x} = hard-matches on client-derived IDs crash the LiveView on stale/deleted records - shopping_live.ex:134,150,177,442; meal_planner_live.ex:277; voting_live.ex:132-137; voting.ex:76-77. Plus raw String.to_integer/Date.from_iso8601!/Decimal.new/String.to_existing_atom on phx-value params throughout (meal_planner_live.ex:313,152,457,506; shopping_live.ex:395) - crafted messages crash the session. Also try/rescue blocks inside LiveViews violating the boundary rule (voting_live.ex:37,58,88; recipes_live.ex:53-78; item_handlers.ex:306) and the bang-vs-tuple asymmetry in item_handlers.ex:102-110. Introduce a small param-validation boundary helper and convert hard-matches to case with flash on error.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared param parsing helper returning error tuples; listed call sites converted
- Stale-ID events produce a flash, not a crash (tests for shopping + meal planner)
- No try/rescue remains in LiveView modules
- item_handlers create path uses non-bang call with real actor like its update path

title: confirm_import silently drops failed receipt items but reports them as added
description: receipt_live.ex:696-717 confirm_import issues one update_receipt_item per item in a for loop, discards every result; failures are silent yet the item is still marked :confirmed and counted in the N-items-added flash - user told data was saved that was not. Related same-file pattern: H8 sibling in receipts_live.ex:693,710 hard-matches on list_receipts/Ash.load!.
acceptance_criteria:
- Per-item failures are collected; flash reports actual added vs failed counts
- Failed items not marked confirmed (test)
- receipts_live load paths handle read errors without crashing the LiveView

title: Make confirm_swap transactional (partial-write meal swap + crash)
description: meal_planner_live.ex:640,648 confirm_swap runs two sequential update_meal_plan calls as {:ok, _} = hard-matches inside the with body, no transaction. If the second update fails the first is committed -> half-completed swap (both meals in one slot or an emptied slot) AND a MatchError crash. Wrap in a transaction (or Ash bulk/atomic action) and handle errors as a flash.
acceptance_criteria:
- Swap is atomic: failure leaves both slots unchanged (test)
- No hard-match crash; user sees an error flash

title: Standardize create policies on ActorMemberOfAccount (remove authorize_if always)
description: meal_plan, grocery_item, receipt, shopping_list, meal_plan_template use authorize_if always() on create while recipe/recipe_ingredient/family_* use ActorMemberOfAccount. Verified not cross-tenant exploitable today (tenant server-derived; Account read policy blocks foreign account lookups in manage_relationship) but it is a defense-in-depth gap relying entirely on tenant being set. Standardize all creates on ActorMemberOfAccount.
acceptance_criteria:
- All tenant-scoped resources use ActorMemberOfAccount on create
- Existing create flows (UI + JSON API) still pass
- Background/system creates keep authorize?: false + tenant

title: Decision note: deliberately deferred product directions
description: Record the researched do-NOT-build list with reasons and what each would need if revisited: (1) free-generation of recipes - documented safety failures (near-botulism advice, 36-53 percent nutrition errors); revisit only with RAG-over-vetted-corpus + human review. (2) Live cross-retailer price scraping - a data-ops treadmill vs Trolley.co.uk; revisit only via open APIs like Open Prices. (3) Medical condition-specific planning - needs clinical review + liability framing. (4) Heavy gamification - academic evidence shows short-term effect, weak long-term retention; keep it thin. (5) Grocery-delivery integration (Instacart et al) - US-centric, API-gated, wrong fit for a UK self-hosted household app today. (6) Smart-fridge camera parity - closed hardware, wrong audience. Write as docs/decisions/ note so future sessions do not re-litigate.

title: NFC/QR pantry shelf labels: tap to consume or add
description: Cheap NFC stickers or printed QR on pantry shelves/bins: tap phone -> instant consume-one / add-to-list for that item, no app navigation. Offline-friendly (queues through the existing sync API), very homelab-tinkerer, and another attack on inventory friction for the physical moments barcode and receipts miss (used the last stock cube).

title: iCal calendar feed + prep-ahead reminders
description: Read-only iCal/ICS feed of the household meal plan (subscribe from Google/Apple/Proton calendar - where families actually look), with optional prep-ahead reminders derived from recipe metadata (take mince out of freezer tonight; marinate at lunch). Repeatedly requested across trackers; small surface, big daily-habit integration.

title: Open Food Facts + Open Prices integration (metadata enrichment, opt-in contribution)
description: Open Food Facts gives free barcode-to-product metadata (names, categories, images, nutrition); its Open Prices sibling is an open crowdsourced price API explicitly designed to be fed by receipt scanning. Consume: enrich items and get community shelf prices without building a scraper (research verdict: never try to out-scrape Trolley - a data treadmill we would lose). Contribute: strictly opt-in, per-household toggle, from parsed receipt lines. Also the metadata backbone for the barcode path.

title: Should-I-stock-up signals from price trend + consumption rate
description: For non-perishables: combine an item's personal price history with its observed consumption rate to flag when it is cheap versus its own trend (olive oil is 18 percent below your average and you use a bottle a month - stock up). Small surface (a badge on shopping list items and the item page); high delight-per-effort once price history exists.

title: School lunchbox planner
description: A distinct, underserved family job separate from dinner planning: rotate packed lunches per child across the school week, respecting per-member preferences/allergies (2-state model already exists), avoiding repeats, feeding the shopping list, term-time aware. Very family-app rather than fitness-app framing; no mainstream planner treats lunchboxes as a first-class plan type.

title: Kid mode: report out-of-stock, claim tasks, light points
description: OurHome-style kid participation works for younger kids: a simple scoped mode where a kid can tap we-are-out-of-X (feeding the shopping list), claim a cooking/shopping helper task, and earn parent-defined rewards (Friday pizza pick). Research caveat baked in: gamification evidence is weak for long-term retention - keep it a thin seasoning on real tasks, not a points economy.

title: Repo housekeeping: remove committed cruft, fix CLAUDE.md drift, minor nits
description: (1) git rm --cached: session-ses_43c3.md (342KB agent transcript at repo root), tracked .omc/** and .nwave/des/logs/*.log (committed before the ignore rules); move or delete stale root planning docs (IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, PROGRESS.md, COMPONENT_AUDIT.md, COMPONENT_LIBRARY.md, GEMINI.md). (2) CLAUDE.md drift: documents 2 of 10 registered domains; says PostgreSQL 18+ while compose runs pgvector:pg16. (3) Minor code nits: invalid inline ring-color CSS silently ignored (items_tab.ex:41,211 - Tailwind wants --tw-ring-color); parse_measure regex accepts trailing dot and mangles fractions (recipe_importer.ex:99-108); infer_category never assigns Dairy/Bakery/Frozen/Beverage/Household so seeds land in Pantry (starter_data.ex:48-64); the_meal_db.ex:46-57 random/1 mishandles 200-with-nil-meals; Task.start rescue swallows log_correction errors (item_handlers.ex:294-308); embeddings.ex:126-238 Repo.query! raises inside functions presenting tuple contracts.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tracked cruft removed from the index; planning docs relocated or deleted
- CLAUDE.md domain list and PG version corrected
- Each listed nit fixed or explicitly declined in a note
